experiment: grounded vs ungrounded Haiku reviewer, same partitions/challenger
method: same Haiku reviewer+plain-Haiku-challenger; reviewer change only = grounding bundle (caliper ground, codegraph) + don't-flag ledger + self-refutation. Partitions selected for being FP-heavy in baseline (selection bias).
headline: Grounding cut raw findings 22->9, eliminated all 8 false positives, raised confirm-rate 45%->100%, preserved real-bug yield (9 vs 10), and surfaced a new class of cross-file contract bugs (P03 OpaRegoAdapter).
caveats: n=1, 3 partitions, single run, no variance
selection bias: partitions chosen for being FP-heavy
verifier is Haiku; no Opus adjudication or human label on the grounded set
recall unmeasured (possible false negatives from over-suppression)
next_step: full 20-partition grounded rerun + Opus adjudication on deltas for an unbiased, recall-aware number
